name: Los Angeles: The Glamour of Hollywood Boulevard

stops.name: Hollywood Walk of Fame
stops.shortDescription: Comprising over 2,700 five-pointed terrazzo and brass stars embedded in the sidewalks. It serves as an evolving public monument to the achievements of the entertainment industry.

stops.name: TCL Chinese Theatre
stops.shortDescription: Opened in 1927, this exotic movie palace has hosted countless glamorous film premieres. Its concrete forecourt bears the handprints, footprints, and signatures of Hollywood's biggest legends.

stops.name: Dolby Theatre
stops.shortDescription: The permanent home of the annual Academy Awards ceremonies since 2002. Its grand staircase is lined with glass pillars displaying the names of every Best Picture winner in Oscar history.

stops.name: El Capitan Theatre
stops.shortDescription: A fully restored, opulent 1926 movie palace now operated by The Walt Disney Company. It features elaborate pre-show entertainment and a massive Wurlitzer pipe organ with over 2,500 pipes.

stops.name: Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel
stops.shortDescription: A historic Spanish-style hotel that hosted the very first Academy Awards in 1929. Marilyn Monroe lived here for two years, and her ghost is rumored to still haunt a full-length mirror in the lobby.

stops.name: Madame Tussauds Hollywood
stops.shortDescription: The renowned wax museum where visitors can interact closely with uncannily realistic figures of celebrities. It famously lacks velvet ropes, encouraging guests to touch and pose directly with the displays.
